description_long: This object is a classic British police telephone box, most notably resembling the TARDIS from Doctor Who. It features a tall, rectangular structure with a flat roof topped by a small lantern. The exterior is painted a weathered, dark blue with visible signs of aging and dirt, giving it a vintage, well-used appearance. The front side has a door with multiple small glass window panes and a prominent white sign, while the upper section displays the words 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' in white on a black background. The sides and rear are similarly paneled, with the rear sign text mirrored. The booth is constructed primarily from wood or metal, with glass window panes and a metal or plastic lantern on top.
description: A weathered, dark blue British police telephone box with glass window panes, a lantern on top, and 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' signage. The structure is tall, rectangular, and shows signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main door with glass panes, a white sign, and clear 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' signage above.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front but without the white sign on the door; signage and window arrangement remain visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with mirrored 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' signage and no door or white sign, showing only window panels.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, with window panels and signage but no door or white sign.
attribute_window_configuration: six small rectangular glass panes on each side of the upper section
attribute_signage_or_labeling: 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' in white on black panels above each side; white instruction sign on the front door
